concept.conceptName: Magnesium | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge
Magnesium [Moles/volume] corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma

en.name: Magnesium in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Magnesium is an intracellular cation involved in many enzymatic reactions. Its concentration in blood is measured to investigate hypo- or hypermagnesaemia [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit), as a mass concentration in mg/dL, or in mEq/L [2]. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on two LOINC Hierarchy concepts (one per blood specimen, scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), with their descendants pulled in. After exclusions, the set resolves to five LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma (default specimen for routine biochemistry) - *3012095 / 2601-3 — Magnesium [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code worldwide. - *3001420 / 19123-9 — Magnesium [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *40759058 / 55937-7 — Magnesium [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --post dialysis (LOINC)*: point-in-time measurement immediately after a haemodialysis session. ### Generic blood (specimen unspecified) - *3033836 / 2593-2 — Magnesium [Moles/volume] in Blood (LOINC)* - *3006916 / 21377-7 — Magnesium [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. Conversion: `1 mmol/L ≈ 2.43 mg/dL` (molar mass of Mg = 24.305 g/mol); `1 mEq/L = 0.5 mmol/L` (magnesium is divalent). ## Excluded Concepts The hierarchies above pull in two LOINC concepts that are explicitly excluded: - *3003341 / 27210-4 — Magnesium [Presence] in Blood*: qualitative result (present / absent), not a quantitative measurement. - *40768058 / 65362-6 — Magnesium [Moles/volume] corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma*: derived value (corrected for albumin), not the raw measurement. The set also deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Red Blood Cells specimens** (e.g. `3021770 / 26746-8 — Magnesium [Mass/volume] in Red Blood Cells`): intra-erythrocytic magnesium, specialised research measurement. - **White blood cells specimens**: specialised research measurement. - **Urine, CSF, body fluid and other specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "magnesium", "Mg", "magnesaemia" without further specification: `3012095 / 2601-3 — Magnesium [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source unit is mg/dL**: convert to mmol/L using `1 mg/dL ≈ 0.4114 mmol/L` (i.e. divide by 2.4305). - **Source unit is mEq/L**: convert to mmol/L using `1 mEq/L = 0.5 mmol/L` (magnesium is divalent). - **Do not confuse with**: corrected magnesium (for albumin, separate measurand), intra-erythrocytic magnesium, urinary magnesium. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2601-3 — Magnesium [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/2601-3/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
